lines.raw_text: ## Communication Technology
lines.tagged_text: <null>## Communication Technology</null>

lines.raw_text: **Acoustic communication** is indeed the dominant approach for wireless subsea systems.[1][2][3] [Imenco](https://imencogroup.com) combines acoustic communication expertise with hydraulic systems to provide remotely operated hydraulic systems using long-range wireless acoustic communication.[1] [DiveNet](https://www.divenetgps.com) offers portable, high-performance underwater GPS navigation and voice/data communication powered by patented hydroacoustic technology.[3] [DSPComm's](https://dspcommgen2.com) Aqua Comm solution delivers reliable communications with high data throughput even in harsh conditions, with redundant communication links increasingly becoming standard for critical underwater infrastructure.[2]

lines.raw_text: ## Valve Actuation and Control
lines.tagged_text: <null>## Valve Actuation and Control</null>

lines.raw_text: **Electric actuators** are widely deployed in subsea applications.[4][5] Linear actuators convert rotational motion into straight-line movement for valve control, with oil-filled designs commonly used for extreme pressure conditions.[5] Servo actuators and stepper motor controllers provide precision and power in high-demand subsea environments, while contactless absolute position feedback systems enable real-time monitoring of actuator positions.[5] [WITTENSTEIN](https://motion-control.wittenstein-us.com) uses ISO 13628-qualified electric drive technology for subsea robotics, providing precise torque and speed feedback for online diagnosis and valve condition testing.[4]

lines.raw_text: These modems use blue-green lasers (around 450-550 nm) for better penetration, with ranges reduced in turbid water—e.g., simulations show laser-diode systems reaching only 3.08 m in turbid harbor water at certain power thresholds, compared to 68 m in pure seawater.[1] Hydromea's LUMA™ modems establish links beyond visibility range in murky water, up to 50 m in clear conditions and 10 Mbit/s data rates; the LUMA™ X-UV variant uses UV wavelengths to handle ambient light in shallow, turbid environments.[2][4] Shimadzu's MC500 achieves up to 80 m range with 20 Mbps using green/blue lasers, tested in varying turbidity, and pairs with acoustic modems for hybrid long-range use.[3][6]

lines.raw_text: ## Key Advantages of ROV Inspections
lines.tagged_text: <null>## Key Advantages of ROV Inspections</null>

lines.raw_text: ROVs eliminate the need for costly and risky procedures.[2][7] Traditional inspections often require draining tanks, confining workers to hazardous spaces, or deploying diving teams—all of which disrupt operations and pose safety risks.[2][4] In contrast, ROV inspections keep tanks in service, avoiding downtime for water availability.[4] The cost to perform an ROV inspection is approximately one-third the cost of a diver inspection.[7]

lines.raw_text: 1. **Acoustic Communication:** This is the most common method for underwater data transfer. It uses sound waves, which travel well in water, to transmit data over distances ranging from a few meters to several kilometers. Acoustic modems are widely used in underwater vehicles, sensors, and communication networks.[1][3][4]

lines.raw_text: **High-bandwidth underwater communication over short ranges is typically enabled by optical communication technology.** Underwater optical communication uses light, often in the **blue-green spectrum**, to transmit data at very high speeds[1][2]. This method offers much higher bandwidth compared to traditional underwater acoustic communication, which is slower and more suited for long-range but low-data-rate transmissions[1][2]. However, optical communication underwater is limited to short distances because water absorbs and scatters light, especially over longer ranges or in turbid conditions[1][2].

lines.raw_text: 1. **Use of Advanced Technology:** Implement drones, remotely operated vehicles (ROVs), and autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) for inspections. These technologies can reduce the need for costly human divers and manned vessels.[1][2][3][5][6]

spans.text: Using a wireless ROV (Remotely Operated Vehicle) for tank inspections offers several advantages over a tethered ROV:

lines.raw_text: 1. **Increased Maneuverability:** Without a tether cable, wireless ROVs can move more freely inside confined or complex tank environments, reducing the risk of entanglement or snagging.
lines.tagged_text: <null>1. **Increased Maneuverability:** Without a tether cable, wireless ROVs can move more freely inside confined or complex tank environments, reducing the risk of entanglement or snagging.</null>

spans.text: 1. **Increased Maneuverability:** Without a tether cable, wireless ROVs can move more freely inside confined or complex tank environments, reducing the risk of entanglement or snagging.

lines.raw_text: 2. **Simplified Setup:** Wireless ROVs eliminate the need to manage and deploy a tether cable, making the inspection setup quicker and less cumbersome.
lines.tagged_text: <null>2. **Simplified Setup:** Wireless ROVs eliminate the need to manage and deploy a tether cable, making the inspection setup quicker and less cumbersome.</null>

spans.text: 2. **Simplified Setup:** Wireless ROVs eliminate the need to manage and deploy a tether cable, making the inspection setup quicker and less cumbersome.

lines.raw_text: 3. **Reduced Risk of Damage:** Tether cables can be damaged or cause damage to the tank interior if they get caught or dragged. Wireless ROVs avoid this issue.
lines.tagged_text: <null>3. **Reduced Risk of Damage:** Tether cables can be damaged or cause damage to the tank interior if they get caught or dragged. Wireless ROVs avoid this issue.</null>

spans.text: 3. **Reduced Risk of Damage:** Tether cables can be damaged or cause damage to the tank interior if they get caught or dragged. Wireless ROVs avoid this issue.

lines.raw_text: 4. **Enhanced Safety:** Operators can control the ROV remotely from a safe distance without being physically connected, which can be beneficial in hazardous environments.
lines.tagged_text: <null>4. **Enhanced Safety:** Operators can control the ROV remotely from a safe distance without being physically connected, which can be beneficial in hazardous environments.</null>

spans.text: 4. **Enhanced Safety:** Operators can control the ROV remotely from a safe distance without being physically connected, which can be beneficial in hazardous environments.

lines.raw_text: 5. **Improved Data Transmission Flexibility:** Wireless communication allows for real-time video and data transmission without the physical limitations imposed by tether length.
lines.tagged_text: <null>5. **Improved Data Transmission Flexibility:** Wireless communication allows for real-time video and data transmission without the physical limitations imposed by tether length.</null>

lines.raw_text: Optical systems excel in short-range, high-bandwidth scenarios due to low attenuation of blue/green light in water, enabling real-time HD video and large file transfers, unlike acoustic modems' low speeds.[1][2][4][5][8] Limitations include shorter ranges and sensitivity to water clarity, making them complementary to acoustic tech.[1][7] Commercial examples include **Shimadzu** MC series (tens Mbps, full-duplex[1][2]), **LUMA** by Hydromea (10 Mbps, low power[3][4][6]), and research from **MIT Lincoln Laboratory** (WiFi-like speeds[5]).

lines.raw_text: Query: A way to perform ballast tank inspections without using a tethered cable is to use **untethered underwater drones** or **autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs)**. These devices are equipped with cameras and sensors and can navigate inside ballast tanks independently, capturing video and data for inspection without the need for physical cables[2][5][8]. This method provides greater **maneuverability**, reduces the risk of entanglement, and can streamline the inspection process compared to traditional tethered remotely operated vehicles (ROVs)[2][8].

lines.raw_text: Encapsulated motors improve the maintenance cycle of underwater propulsion units primarily by enhancing their protection and reliability. Here's how:
lines.tagged_text: <null>Encapsulated motors improve the maintenance cycle of underwater propulsion units primarily by enhancing their protection and reliability. Here's how:</null>

spans.text: Encapsulated motors improve the maintenance cycle of underwater propulsion units primarily by enhancing their protection and reliability. Here's how:

lines.raw_text: 1. **Protection from Water and Contaminants:** Encapsulation seals the motor components inside a protective casing, preventing water ingress, salt, and other contaminants from reaching sensitive parts. This minimizes corrosion and damage.[1][2][3]

spans.text: 1. **Protection from Water and Contaminants:** Encapsulation seals the motor components inside a protective casing, preventing water ingress, salt, and other contaminants from reaching sensitive parts. This minimizes corrosion and damage.[1][2][3]

lines.raw_text: 2. **Reduced Wear and Tear:** By isolating the motor from harsh underwater environments, encapsulated motors experience less mechanical wear, which extends their operational life.[1][2][3]
lines.tagged_text: <null>2. **Reduced Wear and Tear:** By isolating the motor from harsh underwater environments, encapsulated motors experience less mechanical wear, which extends their operational life.[1][2][3]</null>

spans.text: 2. **Reduced Wear and Tear:** By isolating the motor from harsh underwater environments, encapsulated motors experience less mechanical wear, which extends their operational life.[1][2][3]

lines.raw_text: 3. **Lower Maintenance Frequency:** Since the motor is well-protected, the intervals between required maintenance checks can be lengthened, reducing downtime and maintenance costs.[1][4]
lines.tagged_text: <null>3. **Lower Maintenance Frequency:** Since the motor is well-protected, the intervals between required maintenance checks can be lengthened, reducing downtime and maintenance costs.[1][4]</null>

lines.raw_text: 4. **Improved Reliability:** Encapsulation helps maintain consistent performance by safeguarding electrical insulation and components, leading to fewer unexpected failures.[1][2]

spans.text: 4. **Improved Reliability:** Encapsulation helps maintain consistent performance by safeguarding electrical insulation and components, leading to fewer unexpected failures.[1][2]

lines.raw_text: 5. **Simplified Maintenance Procedures:** When maintenance is needed, the encapsulated design often allows for easier handling and replacement without extensive disassembly or exposure to the marine environment.[1]

lines.raw_text: 1. **[Hydromea](https://www.hydromea.com)**: A leading Swiss company based in Yverdon-les-Bains specializing in autonomous underwater vehicles, subsea robotics, and high-speed optical underwater communication systems. Their LUMA series enables wireless, real-time high-definition video streaming underwater at depths up to 6,000 meters. Hydromea is also involved in developing global standards for underwater wireless optical communication.

lines.raw_text: The choice of tool depends on factors such as basin size, water clarity, accessibility, and inspection goals. For industrial basins and flooded structures, [ROVs](https://oxmaint.com/industries/government/underwater-rov-robots-government-dam-reservoir-inspection) combined with ultrasonic testing and high-quality video systems are often the most practical and efficient.[1][2][3][4][5]
